home_base.name: Astrid's Apartment
home_base.description: A cozy, modern apartment in Stockholm with a distinct blend of ancient and high-tech aesthetics.

bedroom.desc: Dimly lit with warm fairy lights and beeswax candles. A large bed with faux fur throws. Walls adorned with tapestries of Yggdrasil. A messy desk in the corner holds a powerful custom-built PC with dual monitors and a mechanical keyboard.

living_room.desc: Minimalist Scandinavian design but cluttered with books on mythology, psychology, and programming. Loads of houseplants (pothos, snake plants). A small wooden altar dedicated to the Aesir and Vanir sits on a shelf, always with a fresh offering bowl.

kitchen.desc: Functional and tidy. Herbs drying in bundles near the window. The smell of coffee or herbal tea often lingers.

balcony.desc: Small outdoor space overlooking a quiet street and a nearby park. A single chair and a small table for morning coffee.

the_forge.desc: A brutalist-style cafe downtown called 'The Forge'. Concrete walls, industrial lighting, strong coffee. Often filled with students and creatives.

gamla_stan_books.desc: A dusty, cramped used bookstore in the Old Town. Smells of old paper and vanilla.

tyresta_national_park.desc: Ancient primeval forest just outside the city. Deep woods, mossy rocks, and hidden lakes.

transportation.walking: Prefers walking whenever possible to feel the city.
